disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical aching and weakness in the large proximal muscles of both thighs, buttocks and shoulders, with a heavy, worn-out feeling
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the pain and weakness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's in both thighs, my backside and both shoulders — the same on each side. They ache deeply and feel so heavy and weak that I can barely lift myself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began a few weeks after the patient's cholesterol-lowering tablet dose was increased (roughly four weeks ago)
trigger_question_examples: Have any of your medications changed recently?
When did the muscle symptoms start relative to any medication change?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it — my heart specialist doubled the strength of my cholesterol tablet about a month ago, and the aching came on a few weeks after that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient recently started an antibiotic for a chest infection, taken over the same period as the increased cholesterol tablet
trigger_question_examples: Are you taking any other medicines right now, including anything new?
Have you started any antibiotics or short courses of medication recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I've also just started an antibiotic for a chest infection. I've been taking it at the same time as my cholesterol tablet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Urine remains a normal clear-yellow colour; no dark, brown, tea- or cola-coloured urine
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any dark or tea-coloured urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my water still looks a normal clear yellow — I haven't seen it go dark or brown.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Now unable to stand from a chair or the toilet without physical help from the spouse; feels very unsteady and frightened of falling
trigger_question_examples: Can you still stand and walk on your own?
How is this affecting getting around at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't get up from the chair or the toilet by myself anymore — my wife has to pull me up. I feel very shaky and I'm scared I'll fall.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No numbness, no pins-and-needles, and no loss of bladder or bowel control
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness, tingling, or loss of feeling?
Any problems controlling your bladder or bowels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no numbness or tingling anywhere, and no trouble with my bladder or bowels — it's purely the aching and weakness.
